name: Sabadell, Cal Barata, Togones, Riu Tort – Circular Route
name_original: Sabadell, Cal Barata, Togones, Riu Tort – Ruta Circular
region_intro: Jogging around Sabadell offers access to a network of green spaces and natural areas within and surrounding the city. The region features extensive urban parks like Parc de Catalunya and Can Gambús, providing varied terrain for running. Riverine paths along the Ripoll River and trails extending into the surrounding countryside offer additional options for runners. The landscape includes a mix of flat parkland, gentle inclines, and dirt paths. Best jogging routes around Sabadell The most popular jogging route is Sabadell, Cal Barata, Togones, Riu Tort – Circular Route, a 5.1 miles (8.2 km) trail that takes 56 minutes to complete. This route offers a circular path through varied natural areas. Another top favourite among local runners is Molí d'en Torrella – Ripoll River Park loop from Sabadell Centre, a difficult 9.9 miles (16.0 km) path. This route follows the Ripoll River, featuring natural scenery and historical mills. Local runners also love the Molí d'en Fontanet Footbridge – Molí d'en Torrella loop from Sabadell, a 5.0 miles (8.0 km) trail leading through the Ripoll River area, often completed in about 51 minutes. Jogging around Sabadell is defined by extensive urban parks, riverine paths, and trails connecting to the surrounding countryside. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy park loops to longer, more challenging runs. The routes in Sabadell are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 80 reviews. More than 3000 runners have used komoot to explore Sabadell's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Sabadell?
A: Sabadell offers a wide variety of running options, with over 150 routes available for you to explore. These range from easy park loops to more challenging trails through natural areas.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running trails in Sabadell?
A: You'll find a diverse range of terrain. Urban parks like Parc de Catalunya feature extensive lawns and dirt paths, while riverine paths along the Ripoll River offer natural scenery. Trails extending into the surrounding countryside provide a mix of flat parkland, gentle inclines, and varied surfaces.

Q: What do other runners say about the routes in Sabadell?
A: The running routes in Sabadell are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 80 reviews. Runners often praise the varied terrain, the accessibility of green spaces, and the scenic views, especially in areas like Parc de Catalunya.

Q: Are there easy running routes for beginners in Sabadell?
A: Yes, Sabadell has several easy running routes perfect for beginners. You can find around 10 routes specifically graded as easy, often within the city's well-maintained parks, offering gentle inclines and comfortable surfaces.

Q: Can I find longer running trails for more challenging runs?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a greater challenge, Sabadell offers many longer and more difficult routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31538811" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molí d'en Torrella – Ripoll River Park loop from Sabadell Centre&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 9.9-mile (16.0 km) path that follows the Ripoll River, providing a substantial workout amidst natural scenery.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Sabadell?
A: Yes, many of Sabadell's running routes are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e963550234/sabadell-cal-barata-togones-riu-tort-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sabadell, Cal Barata, Togones, Riu Tort – Circular Route&lt;/a&gt;, which is 5.1 miles (8.2 km) and takes you through varied natural areas.

Q: Which running routes offer scenic views or natural features?
A: Many routes in Sabadell boast scenic beauty. Parc de Catalunya offers incredible views of distant mountain ranges like Montseny, Montserrat, and La Mola. The Parc Fluvial del Ripoll, running alongside the Ripoll River, is also ideal for tranquil runs amidst nature, featuring bridges and old mills. For a different perspective, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31903401" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Catalonia Park loop from Can Feu | Gràcia&lt;/a&gt; provides a great way to experience the city's largest park.

Q: What landmarks or points of interest can I see while running in Sabadell?
A: Sabadell's routes often pass by interesting natural and historical points. Along the Ripoll River, you might encounter old mills. For natural features, consider routes near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2630086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pi d'en Xandri&lt;/a&gt; or explore the broader &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1575768" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gallecs Rural Area&lt;/a&gt;. Some trails also offer glimpses of distant peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/239333" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mola&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly running trails in Sabadell?
A: Many of Sabadell's extensive green spaces and natural parks, such as Parc de Catalunya and Parc Fluvial del Ripoll, are generally dog-friendly. It's always a good idea to keep your dog on a leash and check local regulations for specific areas within the parks.

Q: Are there family-friendly running routes in Sabadell?
A: Yes, Sabadell's urban parks like Parc de Catalunya and Can Gambús Park are excellent for family-friendly runs. They offer wide, accessible paths and green areas, making them suitable for all ages and fitness levels. These parks often include amenities like picnic areas and playgrounds.

Q: Is parking available near the running routes in Sabadell?
A: Given Sabadell's focus on accessible green spaces, parking is generally available near major parks and trailheads. Urban parks often have dedicated parking facilities, and routes extending into the countryside may have roadside parking options. It's advisable to check specific route details for the most convenient parking spots.

Q: How accessible are Sabadell's running routes by public transport?
A: Sabadell's commitment to green spaces means many parks are within a ten-minute walk of residential areas, making them highly accessible by public transport. The city's bus network and train stations often provide convenient access to the starting points of various running routes, especially those within the urban core.

Q: Are there places to explore beyond the immediate city parks?
A: Definitely. Sabadell promotes outdoor itineraries known as the 'Rodal de Sabadell,' which connect the urban center with the surrounding countryside. These routes offer a mix of hiking trails, small streams, and rural landscapes, providing opportunities for longer runs and exploration beyond the city's main parks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33926409" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molí d'en Torrella – Hermitage of the Torre del Canonge loop from Sabadell Centre&lt;/a&gt; is a great example of a route that takes you further afield.

Q: Are there any running clubs or dedicated facilities in Sabadell?
A: For dedicated runners, the Cercle Sabadellès 1856 sports and social club provides comprehensive sports facilities, including two dedicated running circuits. You can find more information about their offerings on the &lt;a href="https://visitvalles.com/en/portfolio_page/cercle-sabadelles-1856/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Visit Vallès website&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: Very pleasant and calm road that runs parallel to the GR 173, much more transited. The first and last stretch pass through the middle of the forest and the central section, in spring, offers a fantastic view of the huge meadow that borders.

The hermitage of Togores is a monument in the municipality of Sabadell (Vallès Occidental) protected as a cultural asset of local interest. It is a hermitage and farmhouse possibly from the fourteenth century, located to the left of Ripoll, in the valley of the river Tort, on an esplanade surrounded by holm oaks.
Little path that passes through the forest that joins the Ermita de la Torre del Canonge with the Hermitage of the Virgin of Togores.

What is now a ruined farmhouse has a long history. Before there was a strong house, perhaps with a defense tower. It was the residence of the lords of Togores, owners of the territory called Quadra de Togores. Observing the ruins of the house, one can see a large mansion with a ground floor and two floors, with a square wine press attached to the left body and, to the right, a large cellar, with what had been pumice stone arcades, probably medieval, stolen in the 80s, two circular wine presses and an oven. Attached to the front of the cellar there are other rooms, some with mud walls.

Halfway up the Camí de la Torre del Canonge you will find, on the right hand side, a pleasant path that runs through the middle of meadows and is a good shortcut if you go to the Camí Ral de la Serra or Camí Vell de Castellar. It can also be done with MTB.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of Our Lady of Togores
Hermitage of the Torre del Canonge
